questions.original_question: 路特斯科技的欧洲、中国、美国分别的门店数量占比如何？
questions.answer:
2024年，路特斯科技在全球的门店总数为208家，其分布情况如下：  
- **欧洲**：70家，占比34%，体现了欧洲作为路特斯品牌发源地的重要地位以及公司在欧洲市场的持续扩展。  
- **中国**：50家，占比约24%，显示了中国市场在路特斯全球布局中的重要性。  
- **美国**：49家，占比约23.56%，反映了路特斯科技在美国市场的强劲表现和持续扩展。  

这一分布情况展示了路特斯科技在全球主要市场的均衡布局和战略重点。

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the proportion of Lotus Technology's store count in Europe in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the proportion of Lotus Technology's store count in Europe in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Deliveries of lifestyle vehicles saw a remarkable 126% year-on-year increase in the first nine months of 2024, constituting 52% of the total deliveries. Europe, being the home of the Lotus brand, demonstrated significant growth with a 372% year-on-year increase in vehicle deliveries during this period. The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. By region, deliveries were distributed as follows: Europe accounted for 35%, China for 25%, North America for 18%, and ROW for 22%. Regarding the store distribution, Europe held 34%, China had 25%, North America had 24%, and ROW had 17%.

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first eleven months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 9,871 vehicles in the first eleven months of 2024. As a top-selling British luxury brand, Lotus continued its strong momentum in the European market, which contributed nearly 40% of total deliveries for the eleven months ended November 30, 2024. China, as a new and growing market for Lotus, contributed about 25% of the total deliveries. rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the proportion of Lotus Technology's store count in Europe in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

rag_info.chunk_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology's distribution model is not common in the automotive industry today. In particular, in China, Lotus Technology's vehicles are sold either directly to users (rather than through dealerships) or through city partners that act as Lotus Technology's sales agents and collect sales commissions on such sales. As of September 30, 2023, Lotus Technology had 206 stores in its global distribution network. Lotus Technology plans to further expand its physical sales network through a balanced combination of self-operated stores and partner stores. This planned expansion may not have the desired effect of increasing sales and enhancing brand recognition in a cost-efficient manner. Lotus Technology may need to invest significant capital and management resources to operate existing self-operated stores and open new ones, and there can be no assurance that Lotus Technology will be able to improve the operational efficiency of its self-operated stores. Besides, Lotus Technology is in the process of integrating some stores from Lotus UK into its retail network pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, and there can be no assurance that the process will be smooth and on time. Lotus Technology's direct-to-consumer approach to vehicle distribution is relatively new and its long-term effectiveness is unproven, especially in China. This approach thus subjects Lotus Technology to substantial risks as it requires, in the aggregate, significant expenditures and provides for slower expansion of its distribution and sales systems as compared to the traditional dealership system. For example, Lotus Technology will not be able to utilize long-established sales channels.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of

summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Deliveries of lifestyle vehicles saw a remarkable 126% year-on-year increase in the first nine months of 2024, constituting 52% of the total deliveries. Europe, being the home of the Lotus brand, demonstrated significant growth with a 372% year-on-year increase in vehicle deliveries during this period. The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. By region, deliveries were distributed as follows: Europe accounted for 35%, China for 25%, North America for 18%, and ROW for 22%. Regarding the store distribution, Europe held 34%, China had 25%, North America had 24%, and ROW had 17%.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024**

In the first nine months of 2024, the Company achieved significant growth, delivering a total of 7,617 units, representing a remarkable 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Deliveries were well-distributed across various regions, with each contributing between 18% to 35% to the overall total, supported by an extensive network of over 200 stores globally. The European market stood out with an exceptional 372% YoY increase in deliveries, while the Rest of the World experienced a 110% growth. Notably, new deliveries commenced for the Emira model in South Africa and India, and the Eletre model in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. Additionally, the unveiling of the Eletre Carbon variant for North America is anticipated, with deliveries expected to start in the first half of 2025.

Sales figures for the period included 3,983 units of "Lifestyle SUV and Sedan," reflecting a 126% increase from 1,762 units in 2023, and 3,634 units of "Sportscars," which marked a 149% rise from 1,459 units in the previous year. Regionally, Europe accounted for 2,683 units (35% share) in 2024, a significant increase from 568 units (18% share) in 2023. China recorded 1,880 units (25% share), a slight decline from 1,993 units (62% share) in 2023. North America reported 1,665 units (22% share) with no prior data for comparison, while the Rest of the World achieved 1,389 units (18% share), up from 660 units (20% share) in 2023.

Financially, the Company reported revenue of 19 units for the first nine months of 2024, representing a substantial 217% YoY increase from 6 units in 2023. Revenue from Lotus increased to 8 units, reflecting a 100% rise, while revenue from other customers surged to 11 units, a remarkable 450% increase from 2 units in 2023. Furthermore, Lotus Robotics secured contracts valued at approximately $130 million for intelligent driving solutions with various automakers, significantly enhancing its technological footprint within the industry.

Overall, the first nine months of 2024 demonstrated the Company's robust growth trajectory, marked by increased deliveries, expanded market presence, and substantial revenue growth, positioning it favorably for future developments.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,194 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology
Reports Unaudited First Quarter 2024 Financial Result 
summary: Lotus Tech, a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, reported impressive financial results for the first quarter of 2024, with revenues reaching $173 million, reflecting an 811% year-on-year growth and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered 2,194 vehicles, with over 75% of these sales occurring in global markets outside of China. This quarter marked the launch of the Emeya, Lotus's first electric hyper-grand tourer, and the introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service, which offers premium customization options for its vehicles. The company's direct-to-customer sales model in China led to over 100% growth in deliveries, while the Emira, a traditional sports car, began deliveries in the US. With a robust global sales network of over 200 stores, Lotus Tech is expanding its reach into new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas, and plans to introduce additional models throughout the year. CEO Qingfeng Feng emphasized the company's commitment to innovation and its Vision80 plan, highlighting the effectiveness of its global strategy in driving growth in key luxury markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With a global sales network of over 200 stores in prime locations around the world, Lotus Technology continues to leverage its global distribution footprint for international expansion. After China and Europe, Lotus Technology began taking orders for the Eletre in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas in the first quarter. Lotus Technology also expects to begin expanding Emeya sales to new markets in the third quarter of the year. "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence, Lotus Technology is making steady progress on delivering Lotus Tech’s Vision80 plan," said Lotus Technology CEO Qingfeng Feng. "Lotus Technology's global strategy continues to drive growth and success as the company expands its presence and sales across key luxury markets worldwide. Lotus Technology is encouraged by the positive feedback received in these new markets and looks forward to rolling out new models to new geographies throughout the year."

[Table Level]  
- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand with Racing Heritage  
- **Table Summary:** This table represents a chronological history of significant milestones and achievements by Lotus in motorsports and automotive design. Key events include early wins in Le Mans, Formula 1, and Indy 500, as well as recent industry recognitions and awards. The table highlights both competitive and design excellence over multiple decades.  
- **Context:** Lotus positions itself as a legacy brand with a rich automotive and racing history, as evidenced by numerous motorsport championships and innovative products.  
- **Special Notes:** Achievements are tied to specific years and vehicle types. The vehicle names (such as Type 18 and Lotus Evija) and awards (e.g., FIA World Champions, Luxury Briefing Awards) are integral to understanding the brand's legacy.  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1 (1957):** The Lotus Eleven achieved its first victory at Le Mans in the 750cc Index Class.  
- **Row 2 (1960):** The Lotus Type 18 secured its first Grand Prix win at Monte Carlo.  
- **Row 3 (1963):** The Lotus Type 25 earned Lotus its first Formula 1 Constructors' Championship as well as the first Formula 1 Drivers' Championship.  
- **Row 4 (1965):** The Lotus Type 38 marked Lotus's first Indy 500 victory.  
- **Row 5 (1978):** The Lotus Sunbeam celebrated its first World Rally Constructors' Championship.  
- **Row 6 (2019):** Lotus was awarded the title "Luxury brand of the year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards.  
- **Row 7 (2020):** The Lotus Evija won the 2020 MUSE Global Design Awards.  
- **Row 8 (2023):** The Lotus Eletre was recognized as the 2023 GQ SUV of the Year.  
- **Row 9 (2024):** Carwow named Lotus the "Brand of the Year," bolstering its automotive excellence reputation.  

[Achievements Summary]  
- **FIA Formula 1 Constructors’ World Champions:** 7 titles.  
- **FIA Formula 1 Drivers’ World Championships:** 6 titles.  
- **FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins:** 81 victories.  
- **Le Mans Wins (In Class):** 9 victories.  
- **Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win:** 1 victory.  
- **FIA World Rally Championships:** 1 title.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. This growth trajectory is supported by a shift towards higher-margin operations and an asset-light business model.

The brand's legacy in motorsports is highlighted by its historical achievements, including multiple championships and prestigious race wins, reinforcing its reputation in the automotive industry. Lotus is also focused on expanding its sales network, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025, alongside enhancing vehicle availability for models like the Emira and Eletre.

Despite these advancements, Lotus Technology faces challenges reflected in its financial statements, including a net loss of $258 million in Q1 2024, which marks a significant increase from the previous year. The company’s balance sheet shows a notable rise in total assets, alongside increased liabilities, indicating ongoing investments and operational costs.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

[Table Level]  
- **Table Title**: Expanding Global Presence and Market Penetration  
- **Table Summary**: This table visually illustrates the expansion of Lotus' global presence through the growth of its retail locations and the increasing market penetration of its key vehicle models in the global sales network. The left section tracks store count growth from 2023 to projected values for 2024 and 2025, while the right section highlights model-specific availability as a percentage of stores over the same period.  
- **Context**: The table underscores the strategic initiatives aligning with Lotus' Vision80 strategy, focused on enhancing global distribution, increasing sales penetration for all its key models, and supporting the transition to intelligent luxury electric vehicles.  
- **Special Notes**: A footnote indicates that the percentage penetration values are approximate, calculated based on the ratio of stores in markets where models are available versus total store count.

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1 (Store Count in 2023)**: In 2023, Lotus operated 215 global retail stores, establishing a solid foundation for its market presence.  
- **Row 2 (Projected Store Count in 2024)**: The store count is expected to increase to approximately 250 by the end of 2024, reflecting steady growth in global availability.  
- **Row 3 (Projected Store Count in 2025)**: By 2025, the total number of stores is projected to exceed 300, further expanding Lotus' global footprint.  
- **Row 4 (Penetration of Emira in 2023)**: In 2023, the Emira (ICE Sportscar) was available in 90% of Lotus’ retail stores, signifying strong penetration relative to Lotus' global distribution.  
- **Row 5 (Penetration of Emira in 2024 and 2025)**: By 2024 and 2025, the Emira is projected to reach 100% store coverage, achieving full availability across all markets.  
- **Row 6 (Penetration of Eletre in 2023)**: In 2023, the Eletre (BEV E-SUV) achieved a 60% penetration rate in available retail stores globally.  
- **Row 7 (Penetration of Eletre in 2024 and 2025)**: By 2024 and 2025, the Eletre’s penetration is expected to reach 100%, aligning with increased store expansion and market focus on BEVs.  
- **Row 8 (Penetration of Emeya in 2023)**: The Emeya (BEV E-Sedan) was not available in any stores in 2023, as it had not yet been introduced to the market.  
- **Row 9 (Penetration of Emeya in 2024)**: In 2024, the Emeya is projected to penetrate 50% of Lotus' retail markets globally, reflecting its initial introduction to the sales network.  
- **Row 10 (Penetration of Emeya in 2025)**: By 2025, the Emeya is expected to reach full availability in 100% of Lotus' retail locations, mirroring Lotus’ comprehensive market expansion and penetration plan.

metadata.title_summary:
title: FY2024 OUTLOOK 
summary: The summary highlights Lotus' strategic growth in global retail presence and market penetration as part of its Vision80 strategy, which focuses on enhancing distribution and transitioning to intelligent luxury electric vehicles. In 2023, Lotus operated 215 retail stores, with plans to increase this number to around 250 by the end of 2024 and over 300 by 2025, demonstrating significant expansion. 

Model-specific penetration rates show that the Emira (ICE Sportscar) was available in 90% of stores in 2023, with full coverage expected by 2024 and 2025. The Eletre (BEV E-SUV) started at a 60% penetration rate and is also projected to reach 100% by 2024 and 2025, reflecting a strong emphasis on battery electric vehicles. The Emeya (BEV E-Sedan), which was not available in 2023, is anticipated to penetrate 50% of retail markets in 2024, with complete availability expected by 2025.

Additionally, in 2024, Lotus celebrated Lotus Day with the launch of the limited-edition Evija Fittipaldi and showcased the Racecar Type 66, marking a significant milestone in its design history. The event also included the commencement of Emeya deliveries, which offer customers immersive experiences, including professional racer training and track racing opportunities. This comprehensive approach underscores Lotus' commitment to expanding its market presence and ensuring widespread availability of its innovative vehicle models.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In 2024, Lotus Technology's Day featured the debut of the Evija Fittipaldi limited-edition with track driving in China. The Racecar Type 66 had its second public display, showcasing a dream design from half a century ago that has come to reality. Emeya delivery commenced, providing an immersive experience of professional racer training and track racing.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A PIVOTAL YEAR DELIVERING LOTUS'VISION8O STRATEGY 
summary: In 2023, Lotus made significant strides in its transition to electric mobility, with the BEV Lifestyle model representing 63% of total deliveries in its first year. The brand expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, a 46-location increase from the previous year, supporting the Vision80 strategy alongside a balanced distribution network. The appointment of nichLI as the exclusive global distributor further bolstered its market presence. 

The year also marked the 75th anniversary of Lotus, celebrated with the launch of the Eletre, the brand's first all-electric hyper SUV, during an event that included a parade of over 600 Lotus drivers at an F1 racetrack, showcasing the company’s heritage and future in electric vehicles.

Financially, Lotus reported impressive growth metrics for 4Q23 and FY2023. Deliveries surged by 110% in 4Q23 to 3,749 units, culminating in a total of 6,970 units for FY2023, a remarkable leap from just 7 units in FY2022. Revenue for 4Q23 reached $361 million, a 92% increase from the previous quarter, with FY2023 revenue soaring to $679 million from $9 million in FY2022. 

Despite rising costs, with 4Q23 cost of sales at $293 million (up 83% QoQ) and FY2023 totaling $577 million, gross profit also saw substantial growth, reaching $68 million in 4Q23 and $102 million for the year. The gross profit margin improved to 19% in 4Q23, while net losses for the quarter and the year were $(224) million and $(750) million, respectively, reflecting ongoing investments in growth.

Looking ahead to FY2024, Lotus anticipates continued growth in deliveries and advancements in its evolution as an intelligent luxury mobility provider. The financial metrics for FY2022 are considered "not comparable" due to the initiation of BEV deliveries in 2023, and EBIT and adjusted EBITDA figures are provided as non-GAAP measures with further details available in the appendix.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

title: Inspection of Books and Records 
summary: The chapter on "Inspection of Books and Records" outlines the protocols established by the board of directors regarding shareholder access to the company's financial documents. The board holds the authority to define the conditions under which shareholders may inspect the company's accounts and books, including the extent of access, timing, location, and specific regulations governing such inspections. 

It is important to note that shareholders do not possess an inherent right to inspect company documents. Access is contingent upon either legal mandates, explicit authorization from the board, or approval through a special resolution passed by the shareholders. This framework ensures that the inspection process is regulated and aligned with both corporate governance and legal requirements.

In summary, the board of directors plays a crucial role in determining the parameters for document inspection, thereby safeguarding the company's interests while also adhering to applicable laws that may grant shareholders certain rights.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Overview**

Lotus is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, renowned for its iconic British brand and over 70 years of racing heritage, which includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles. The company specializes in luxury lifestyle vehicles designed for daily use and has been positioned as a luxury lifestyle BEV maker since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017. This acquisition has provided significant support in manufacturing, supply chain management, and research and development.

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is anticipated to experience substantial growth, projected at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, reaching nearly 1.9 million units. Currently, the market is underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) models. As an early mover in this sector, Lotus aims to launch its E-segment BEV model ahead of competitors and achieve a 100% BEV portfolio by 2027.

The company’s first fully electric model, the Eletre, was launched in 2022 and features an 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture. The Eletre offers three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0-100 km/h acceleration in just 2.95 seconds. Deliveries for the Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023, with plans for global expansion.

Following the Eletre, Lotus introduced its second vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Additionally, the company plans to launch two more BEV models: a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026. All BEV models are manufactured through a partnership with Geely Holding at a new facility in Wuhan, China, which has an annual production capacity of 150,000 units. This collaboration allows Lotus to scale operations and maintain flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, the company delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, including both lifestyle and sports cars, and employed 3,180 full-time staff, with 56.7% of the workforce dedicated to research and development. 

This strategic positioning and robust growth plan underscore Lotus's commitment to becoming a leader in the luxury BEV market while leveraging its rich heritage and innovative capabilities.

title: Employees 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Employee Allocation and Management Practices (as of June 30, 2024)**

As of June 30, 2024, the company employs a total of 2,989 individuals, with a significant majority allocated to various functional areas as follows: 

- **Research and Development**: 1,631 employees (54.6%)
- **Marketing and Sales**: 755 employees (25.3%)
- **Supply Chain**: 204 employees (6.8%)
- **Functional Support**: 399 employees (13.3%)

This distribution underscores the company's strategic focus on innovation and market engagement, which are critical in the competitive landscape. To attract and retain qualified personnel, the company offers competitive compensation packages and fosters a supportive environment that promotes self-development. This approach has been instrumental in maintaining a stable core management team.

In adherence to local regulations, the company participates in various employee social security plans mandated by municipal and provincial governments. These plans encompass pension, unemployment insurance, maternity insurance, work-related injury insurance, medical insurance, and housing insurance. Contributions to these benefit plans are calculated as a percentage of employee salaries, bonuses, and allowances, subject to limits set by local authorities. Bonuses are discretionary and are determined based on individual and overall business performance.

The company utilizes standard labor contracts and confidentiality agreements, ensuring compliance with legal requirements. To date, there have been no significant labor disputes, reflecting effective employee relations. Additionally, the company plans to continue its practice of granting share-based incentive awards, which serve to motivate employees and align their contributions with the company's growth and development objectives.

Overall, the company's employee management practices and allocation strategies are designed to support its business goals while ensuring compliance with regulatory frameworks and fostering a positive work environment.

Founded in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has transitioned from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, especially after Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in 2017. The company plans to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, with additional models like the Emeya and a D-segment SUV set for future release.

Lotus emphasizes its R&D capabilities as a competitive advantage, leveraging its proprietary technologies and advanced driver assistance systems. The company operates five R&D facilities across the U.K., Germany, and China, fostering collaboration among skilled teams. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, which allows for scalability and flexibility while minimizing capital investments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, featuring premium stores and a global digital platform that includes a virtual showroom and customization options. The brand has established a global distribution network, with plans to expand its store count from 215 to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, reflecting its commitment to leading the electrification of luxury vehicles while maintaining strong ties to its British heritage.

NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Permission, Review and Filing Required from the Authorities in Mainland China Relating to Overseas Offering 
summary: The regulatory landscape for China-based companies seeking to raise capital offshore has become increasingly stringent, reflecting the PRC government's intensified oversight. This trend was notably marked by the issuance of the "Opinions on Severely Cracking Down on Illegal Securities Activities" on July 6, 2021, which underscored the need for enhanced supervision of overseas listings. A key aspect of this oversight involves the establishment of regulatory systems aimed at mitigating risks associated with cybersecurity and data privacy.

Effective February 15, 2022, the revised Measures for Cybersecurity Review require online platform operators that process data impacting national security—specifically those with over one million users—to undergo a cybersecurity review prior to any overseas listing. This regulatory framework is part of a broader effort to ensure that companies comply with national security standards.

On February 17, 2023, the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) introduced the Overseas Listing Trial Measures, which mandate that companies, such as Lotus Tech, complete specific filing procedures before pursuing a listing on platforms like Nasdaq. Lotus Tech successfully submitted its filing, which was concluded on February 8, 2024. The Overseas Listing Filing Rules stipulate that issuers must file for various actions, including additional securities issuance and changes in listing status, and are required to report significant events within three business days.

As of the date of the prospectus, Lotus Tech has not encountered any cybersecurity reviews or sanctions from PRC authorities, aside from inquiries related to its filing. Legal counsel for Lotus Tech suggests that while the company believes it must file with the CSRC after issuing American Depositary Shares (ADSs), it does not anticipate needing a cybersecurity review for this specific offering. However, the evolving nature of regulatory interpretation and potential legal changes could result in the need for additional approvals or reviews.

Failure to secure the necessary permissions or misinterpretation of regulatory requirements could lead to significant costs for Lotus Tech and potential sanctions, adversely affecting its business operations, ability to attract foreign investment, and the overall value of its securities. For a comprehensive understanding of the regulatory complexities and their potential impacts on offshore offerings, stakeholders are advised to refer to the "Risk Factors" section of the documentation.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017, we have successfully transitioned into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, benefiting from Geely's support across various operational areas.

Our flagship model, the Eletre, is a luxury E-segment SUV that showcases our innovative 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R stands out with an impressive 905 horsepower, achieving acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds, and offers a WLTP range of 490 km, complemented by rapid charging capabilities. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in 2023, and we launched our second model, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

The achievements reflect Lotus Technology’s steady transition from a traditional sportscar maker to an intelligent luxury mobility provider. Deliveries by Model Type

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology's distribution model is not common in the automotive industry today. In particular, in China, Lotus Technology's vehicles are sold either directly to users (rather than through dealerships) or through city partners that act as Lotus Technology's sales agents and collect sales commissions on such sales. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 215 stores in its global network. distribution network. Lotus Technology plans to further expand its physical sales network through a balanced combination of company-operated stores and partner stores. This planned expansion may not have the desired effect of increasing sales and enhancing brand recognition in a cost-efficient manner. Lotus Technology may need to invest significant capital and management resources to operate existing company-operated stores and open new ones, and there can be no assurance that Lotus Technology will be able to improve the operational efficiency of its company-operated stores. Lotus Technology's direct-to-consumer approach to vehicle distribution is relatively new and its long-term effectiveness is unproven, especially in China. This approach thus subjects Lotus Technology to substantial risks as it requires, in the aggregate, significant expenditures and provides for slower expansion of Lotus Technology's distribution and sales systems as compared to the traditional dealership system. For example, Lotus Technology will not be able to utilize long-established sales channels developed through a dealership system to increase its sales volume. Moreover, Lotus Technology will be competing with automakers with well-established distribution channels, and it may not be able to satisfy customer expectations.

metadata.title_summary:
title: We may not be able to expand our physical sales network cost-efficiently. Our distribution model is different from the currently predominant distribution model for automakers, and its long-term viability is unproven. 
summary: The company employs a distinctive distribution model in the automotive industry, particularly in China, by selling vehicles directly to consumers or through city partners acting as sales agents, rather than relying on traditional dealerships. As of December 31, 2023, it operates 215 stores globally and plans to expand its network of both company-operated and partner stores. However, there are concerns regarding the effectiveness and cost-efficiency of this expansion in boosting sales and brand recognition. Significant investments in capital and management resources will be necessary to manage existing and new stores, with no assurance of improved operational efficiency in company-operated locations.

This direct-to-consumer approach, while innovative, carries risks such as high expenditures and a slower growth rate compared to conventional dealership methods. The company may struggle to compete with established automakers that have robust distribution networks, potentially affecting sales volume. Additionally, while city partners offer a sales partnership opportunity, the company faces challenges in finding and retaining experienced partners capable of managing stores effectively. Although the company provides training and sets service standards, it has limited control over partner operations, which could lead to issues like poor customer service or misconduct that may damage the brand's reputation. Furthermore, disputes with city partners could result in financial losses and operational disruptions, compounding the risks associated with this distribution strategy.

Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,194 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where Lotus Technology operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology
Reports Unaudited First Quarter 2024 Financial Result 
summary: Lotus Tech, a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, reported impressive financial results for the first quarter of 2024, with revenues reaching $173 million—a remarkable 811% increase year-on-year. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 18%, supported by its asset-light model and high-margin sectors like R&D services and accessories. During this period, Lotus delivered 2,194 vehicles, with over 75% of these sales occurring in global markets outside of China. The company launched its first electric hyper-grand tourer, the Emeya, and expanded its product lineup to four models, while also beginning customer deliveries of the Emira sports car in the U.S. 

Lotus Tech's direct-to-customer sales model in China saw over 100% growth in deliveries compared to the previous year. The company is leveraging its extensive global sales network, which includes over 200 stores, to facilitate international growth and has begun taking orders for the Eletre model in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas. CEO Qingfeng Feng emphasized the company's commitment to its Vision80 plan, highlighting the effectiveness of its global strategy and expressing optimism about the positive reception in new markets and the launch of additional models throughout the year. Additionally, Lotus introduced its Chapman Bespoke service, offering premium customization options for its vehicles, further enhancing its luxury appeal.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

title: 1. How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year?
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology demonstrated exceptional growth, reporting total revenue of $398 million, which reflects a remarkable year-on-year increase of 206%. The company's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained robust, exceeding $100,000, and significantly outpacing growth in both the automotive industry and the luxury car segment. This surge in revenue was accompanied by an improvement in gross margin, which rose to 13%, up from 5% during the same period in 2023.

Lotus achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, marking a staggering 239% increase year-on-year, thereby establishing itself as the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car manufacturers. Deliveries were well-distributed across major markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing between 20-30% to the total.

The company has also embarked on strategic collaborations with leading automotive brands, which are expected to enhance technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and technology development services. The growth in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware is anticipated to create additional opportunities for paid software subscription revenue.

Furthermore, the introduction of the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service has allowed Lotus to offer personalized options and designer customizations, catering to evolving customer demands. This initiative is expected to strengthen the brand's luxury positioning and positively influence long-term profit margins.

Overall, Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of 2024 reflects strong financial growth, strategic market positioning, and a commitment to enhancing customer experience, setting a solid foundation for continued success in the luxury automotive sector.

title: Lotus Technology
Reports Unaudited First Quarter 2024 Financial Result 
summary: Lotus Tech, a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, reported impressive financial results for the first quarter of 2024, with revenues reaching $173 million—a remarkable 811% increase year-on-year. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 18%, supported by its asset-light model and high-margin sectors like R&D services and accessories. During this period, Lotus delivered 2,194 vehicles, with over 75% of these sales occurring in global markets outside of China. The company launched its first electric hyper-grand tourer, the Emeya, and expanded its product lineup to four models, while also beginning customer deliveries of the Emira sports car in the U.S. 

Lotus Tech's direct-to-customer sales model in China saw over 100% growth in deliveries compared to the previous year. The company is leveraging its extensive global sales network, which includes over 200 stores, to facilitate international growth and has begun taking orders for the Eletre model in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas. CEO Qingfeng Feng emphasized the company's commitment to its Vision80 plan, highlighting the effectiveness of its global strategy and expressing optimism about the positive reception in new markets and the launch of additional models throughout the year. Additionally, Lotus introduced its Chapman Bespoke service, offering premium customization options for its vehicles, further enhancing its luxury appeal.
